title: Inheritance tree of classes should not be too deep
description: Inheritance tree of classes should not be too deepAnalyze your codeadaptability-focusedmaintainabilityCode SmellarchitectureWhy is this an issue?More InfoInheritance is one of the most valuable concepts in object-oriented programming. It’s a way to categorize and reuse code by creating collections of attributes and behaviors called classes, which can be based on previously created classes.But abusing this concept by creating a deep inheritance tree can lead to complex and unmaintainable source code. Often, an inheritance tree becoming too deep is the symptom of systematic use of "inheritance" when other approaches like "composition" would be better suited.This rule raises an issue when the inheritance tree, starting fromObject, has a greater depth than is allowed.Available In:Catch issues
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects classes participating in deep inheritance chains (depth > 5 levels)
logic.triggering_conditions: Class declaration explicitly inheriting from another class (indication of inheritance chain)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects deep inheritance trees by identifying classes that explicitly inherit from base classes. While this simplified version cannot calculate exact depth without cross-file semantic analysis, it flags potential deep inheritance for manual review. Pattern matches 'class X : BaseClass'. Excludes interface-only inheritance and classes without explicit base classes. Full depth calculation would require custom function with AST traversal.
